Name: Noor
Age & grade: 11, 6th grade.

Interests & passions:
Robotics club lead — she's currently programming a line-follower with an
Arduino Uno and three IR sensors. Built her own claw arm out of laser-cut MDF
and standard hobby servos. Comfortable wiring a breadboard but still mixes up
analog and digital pins occasionally. Competes in regional badminton (under-12
singles, ranked 4th in her division). Her parents are both mechanical
engineers who emigrated from Pakistan; they push hard on math and expect her
to "skip a year" by 8th grade. She is the family's first English speaker —
Urdu at home — and translates for her grandmother weekly.

Hobbies & media:
- Tinkers with Arduino kits — favorite project is a temperature logger that
  texts her dad when the basement gets too cold.
- Badminton training Tuesdays and Thursdays; tournaments most weekends.
- Watches "How To Mechatronics" YouTube tutorials; rewatches "The Karate Kid".
- Plays Roblox with her cousins in Lahore over video call.

Learning style & teacher notes:
ELL learner — her receptive English is excellent but she sometimes
substitutes a wrong technical term mid-sentence ("electron" for "neutron",
"velocity" for "acceleration") and gets visibly frustrated. Build a short
vocab list at the start of each unit and let her pre-read terms before the
lesson. She over-applies what she's just learned — give a hammer, she'll
treat the next three problems as nails. Counter this by pairing each
worked example with a "doesn't apply because…" non-example. She is
performance-anxious because of her parents' expectations; praise her
*method* on a problem she got wrong, not just her correct answers. Her
robotics fluency is a goldmine for hardware analogies — a state machine
maps cleanly onto her line-follower behavior tree.
